vision: A mobile application that helps individuals manage daily tasks efficiently through a straightforward interface for creating, tracking, and completing personal to-do items.

epics.name: Task Creation
epics.description: Enable users to create new tasks with essential details to capture their daily responsibilities quickly

stories.title: Create task with title
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to create a new task with a title, so that I can capture a responsibility I need to remember
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the task list screen When I tap the add task button and enter a title and confirm Then a new task appears in my task list with the entered title
Given I am creating a new task When I leave the title field empty and try to confirm Then the app prevents task creation and shows a validation message

stories.title: Add optional due date to task
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to add an optional due date when creating a task, so that I can track when tasks need to be completed
acceptance_criteria: Given I am creating a new task When I tap the due date field Then I can select a date from a date picker
Given I am creating a new task When I skip the due date field and confirm Then the task is created without a due date
Given I have created a task with a due date When I view the task in the list Then the due date is displayed alongside the task title

epics.name: Task Completion
epics.description: Allow users to mark tasks as complete to track their progress on daily responsibilities

stories.title: Mark task as complete
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to mark a task as complete, so that I can track which responsibilities I have finished
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an incomplete task in my list When I tap the completion toggle Then the task is marked as complete with a visual indicator
Given I have a completed task When I tap the completion toggle again Then the task is marked as incomplete

epics.name: Task Deletion
epics.description: Enable users to remove tasks they no longer need from their list

stories.title: Delete task from list
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to delete a task from my list, so that I can remove items that are no longer relevant
acceptance_criteria: Given I have a task in my list When I perform the delete action on that task Then the task is removed from the list
Given I perform a delete action When the deletion is triggered Then I am asked to confirm before the task is permanently removed

epics.name: Task List Viewing
epics.description: Provide users with the ability to view and browse their tasks in an organized list format

stories.title: View all tasks in list
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to view all my tasks in a list format, so that I can see everything I need to do at a glance
acceptance_criteria: Given I have tasks saved When I open the app Then I see all my tasks displayed in a scrollable list
Given I have no tasks saved When I open the app Then I see an empty state message indicating no tasks exist

stories.title: Filter to show incomplete tasks only
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to filter my task list to show only incomplete items, so that I can focus on what still needs to be done
acceptance_criteria: Given I have both complete and incomplete tasks When I apply the incomplete filter Then only incomplete tasks are displayed
Given I have the incomplete filter active When I remove the filter Then all tasks are displayed again

stories.title: Filter to show complete tasks only
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to filter my task list to show only complete items, so that I can review what I have accomplished
acceptance_criteria: Given I have both complete and incomplete tasks When I apply the complete filter Then only completed tasks are displayed
Given I have the complete filter active When I remove the filter Then all tasks are displayed again

epics.name: Data Persistence
epics.description: Ensure user tasks are saved and available across app sessions and when offline

stories.title: Persist tasks between app sessions
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want my tasks to be saved between app sessions, so that I don't lose my task list when I close the app
acceptance_criteria: Given I have created tasks When I close and reopen the app Then all my previously created tasks are still visible
Given I have marked tasks as complete When I close and reopen the app Then the completion status is preserved
stories.notes: Walking skeleton - essential for usable app

stories.title: Perform task operations offline
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to create, complete, and delete tasks while offline, so that I can manage my tasks without an internet connection
acceptance_criteria: Given I have no internet connection When I create a new task Then the task is saved locally and appears in my list
Given I have no internet connection When I mark a task as complete or delete it Then the change is saved locally
stories.notes: Based on assumed offline capability requirement

epics.name: Cross-Platform Support
epics.description: Deliver the application on both iOS and Android mobile platforms

stories.title: Access app on iOS device
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to use the app on my iOS device, so that I can manage tasks on my iPhone or iPad
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an iOS device When I download and open the app Then all features function correctly following iOS design guidelines
Given I am using iOS When I interact with the app Then the user experience feels native to the iOS platform
stories.notes: Platform delivery - may be handled via cross-platform framework

stories.title: Access app on Android device
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to use the app on my Android device, so that I can manage tasks on my Android phone or tablet
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an Android device When I download and open the app Then all features function correctly following Android design guidelines
Given I am using Android When I interact with the app Then the user experience feels native to the Android platform
stories.notes: Platform delivery - may be handled via cross-platform framework
